latest.title: How We Built a Virtual Team of 10+ AI Agents
latest.description:
Last Monday evening, over 300 people walked into the AWS office in Singapore for the largest OpenClaw community meetup in Southeast Asia. Engineers, founders, C-suite leaders, students — all in the same room. About half had never attended before.

We were two of the speakers. Annie shared how she runs a team of 10+ AI agents through Telegram. Melanie presented the technical architecture for deploying OpenClaw safely on AWS at scale. This blog is our attempt to put the full story down in writing.

latest.title: Investigue fallas de infraestructura en Azure con AWS DevOps Agent
latest.description:
Recorre el flujo completo para configurar la integración de AWS DevOps Agent con Azure, desplegar una API bancaria de ejemplo, introducir una falla de red en PostgreSQL y lanzar una investigación autónoma cross-
cloud que identifica la causa raíz en minutos — sin credenciales almacenadas ni agentes en Azure.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: Ivy - The World's First Offline-Capable, Proactive AI Tutoring Agent
latest.description: A proactive AI tutoring agent powered by Amazon Bedrock that monitors student progress, initiates outbound voice calls, and runs on-device inference for offline learning addressing educational inequity for millions of Ethiopian students with intermittent connectivity.

latest.title: Conquering Week 3 of the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp: EC2, IAM, Security, and Billing
latest.description: Welcome to my recap of Week 3 of the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026! This week was an incredibly hands-on experience where we dove into some of the most fundamental and crucial services in AWS: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Identity and Access Management (IAM), Security Groups, and Billing.

latest.title: Securing the Cloud: My Week 3 AWS Bootcamp Deep Dive into EC2, IAM, and Billing
latest.description:
Just published a deep dive into my Week 3 progress with the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026! This week was all about moving beyond basic setups to implement production-ready security and governance.

Check out the full article to see the terminal configurations and the final live deployment. ☁️ #AWS #CloudComputing #DevOps

latest.title: AWS Bootcamp (UNILORIN)
latest.description: This article provides a brief, hands-on overview of essential cloud setup tasks on Amazon Web Services. It walks through cost monitoring, identity management, secure server deployment, and basic application hosting using Amazon EC2.

latest.title: 🚀 From Beginner to Deploying a Live Web Server on AWS (My Week 3 Bootcamp Experience)
latest.description:
🚀 Just published my Week 3 bootcamp project where I deployed my first live web server on AWS!

From setting up IAM policies to launching an EC2 instance and hosting a web page with Nginx, this project gave me real hands-on experience in cloud computing.

Would love your feedback 🙌

#AWS #CloudComputing #DevOps #LearningJourney

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: CareLens AI — Transparent Care Guidance When It Matters Most.
latest.description: CareLens is a privacy-first AI platform that helps people understand healthcare costs before seeking treatment. By analyzing de-identified symptoms, location, and optional insurance details, it compares care options like ER, urgent care, or specialists and surfaces real-world price ranges using hospital transparency and CMS data. It doesn’t diagnose—it delivers clear, grounded cost, bill analyzing and care guidance so users can make confident decisions without surprise bills.

latest.title: Data Warehouse Design: Concepts, Architecture & Best Practices
latest.description: Learn data warehouse design through key concepts, structure, and best practices. Build systems that are scalable and efficient for modern data analytics.

trending.title: Introducing Article View Counts on AWS Builder Center
trending.description: You can now see how many builders are reading your articles, giving you a real metric to understand the reach of your content.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: VERA, A Multi-Agent Triage System for Healthcare in Chile
trending.description: A multi-agent healthcare triage system that screens patients in Chilean Spanish, detects crisis signals, and generates clinical reports. Built by two people on AWS for $17/month, designed to integrate with Chile's public health system via HL7 FHIR.

trending.title: Revolutionizing Email Productivity with AI-Powered Automation through CLI
trending.description: As a developer facing email overload, I built a solution for the Q Developer Challenge #1: email management. What began as a simple CLI tool grew into an AI-powered system that has transformed my workflow.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Caligo Dynamics - AI Powered Autonomous Drone Swarm for Emergency Response
trending.description: When a fire breaks out, firefighters often enter buildings blindly, unable to see victims, hazards, or even the way out. This project uses AI-powered drone swarms built with ROS2, PX4, Kiro, and Amazon Bedrock to detect fires, locate civilians, map dangerous environments, and help emergency teams contain fires before lives are lost.

trending.title: Deploying OpenClaw on AWS EC2 - A Developer's Perspective
trending.description: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant that you can deploy on your own infrastructure. This post documents the complete process of deploying OpenClaw on AWS EC2: using a single CloudFormation template, you can have it running in about 15 minutes, integrated with the latest Claude models like Opus 4.6 via Amazon Bedrock. US regions offer one-click deployment; other regions require a few additional configuration steps, all covered in this guide.
